a designer who codes her own ideas. Tanisha Bartakke · Mumbai · 2026

I design interfaces, interactions, and small products — and I ship them. Splitting my time between figuring out what something should feel like and actually building it.

Mumbai, IN B.Tech IT · 3rd Year UMIT · CGPA 8.9 Designer / Developer / Writer
01 · About

a quick introduction.

I sit at the intersection of design and code. I'm the person who sketches the flow on paper, mocks it up, then opens an editor and actually ships it. Designing what I build, building what I design.

What I care about: the small details that make products feel intentional — typography, micro-interactions, how a screen feels when it loads, the words on a button. My toolkit is Figma, Next.js, SwiftUI, Streamlit, and v0 for fast prototyping.

Outside of all that, I run tansssbrews — a coffee Instagram I started for fun and have somehow kept growing. I edit a tech podcast for my college's CSI council. And I'm a certified off-roader. Most of what I'm into rewards patience and obsessing over details, which is probably why I like design.

02 · What I Do

three things, seriously.

/01

Design

UI, UX, and the small details — type, spacing, motion, copy. The way a product feels.

/02

Code

Web (Next.js, Tailwind), iOS (SwiftUI), Python (Streamlit). Modern, fast, prototype-friendly.

/03

Write

Microcopy, content for the CSI council, scripts for Techspirants — words are part of design too.

03 · Selected Work

things I've shipped.

/01 — Wellbeing
↗

TapOut.

live demo solo design

A digital wellbeing app exploring how tone of voice can change behavior. Most screen-time apps shame you into compliance — TapOut lets you choose how it talks to you. Pick the sassy persona and it roasts you when you've doomscrolled too long. Pick the wholesome one and it sounds like a kind friend. Same product logic, two completely different emotional experiences. A study in how copy is design.

 Experience TapOut ↗
Persona Design UX Writing Next.js Tailwind
View on GitHub↗
/02 — FinTech / Trust
↗

Nidhi Rakshak.

team of 3 research-led

A digital vault interface for safeguarding financial records and inheritance information. The core design challenge: how do you build trust into a product where users are storing things their families will one day inherit? Solved through clear role-based dashboards (Account Holder vs Nominee), gentle inactivity-based handoff flows, and an interface that feels more like a bank than a startup.

⤢
⤢
⤢
⤢
Product Design Dual Dashboards Next.js Supabase
View on GitHub↗
/03 — Climate / Data Viz
↗

Carbon Conserve.

published @ ICCC team of 3

A web app that turns abstract climate data into a personal mirror — calculate, track, and visualize your own carbon footprint over time. The design challenge was making sustainability data feel actionable instead of overwhelming. Co-authored an abstract on the project that was selected for publication and presentation at the ICCC Conference, Chandrapur.

⤢
⤢
Data Viz Streamlit Python Pandas
View on GitHub↗
/04 — iOS / Personal Finance
↗

MyMoney.

solo build native iOS

A native iOS app for tracking how money moves between two real-life buckets — your bank account and the cash actually in your wallet. Designed around a "bill receipt" metaphor: each transaction looks like the paper slip you'd save, organized by category. A small, focused tool that does one thing in a way that feels familiar.

⤢
⤢
⤢
⤢
iOS Design SwiftUI Mental Models
View on GitHub↗
/05 — Just for fun
↗

Moon Lander.

weekend build

A small physics-based game I built one weekend in Python. Control thrust and angle to land safely without crashing. Mostly here because tiny, finishable side projects say something honest about how someone likes to make things.

Python Pygame
